answer:
\boxed{F}

While **brain biopsy** (Option F) is the most definitive way to confirm Alzheimer's disease by identifying amyloid plaques and neurofibrillary tangles, it is rarely used due to its invasiveness. **Genetic testing (J)** can identify high-risk mutations but does not confirm a diagnosis in all cases. **PET scans (I)** showing amyloid or tau accumulation are also highly accurate but are not universally accessible or definitive on their own.
